CREATE TABLE date_test (startdate DATE, enddate DATE);
SELECT date_diff('month', startdate, enddate) FROM date_test;
SELECT startdate, enddate, date_diff('day', startdate, enddate) AS day_diff FROM date_test WHERE day_diff > 30;
SELECT startdate, enddate, CASE WHEN date_diff('year', startdate, enddate) > 0 THEN 'Years Apart' ELSE 'Same Year' END AS year_comparison FROM date_test;
SELECT dt.startdate, dt.enddate, date_diff('week', dt.startdate, dt.enddate) AS week_diff FROM date_test dt JOIN another_test at ON at.date_check BETWEEN dt.startdate AND dt.enddate;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM date_test) AS total_rows, SUM(date_diff('month', startdate, enddate)) AS total_months FROM date_test;
